step_by_step_thinking:
Clean-contaminated wounds, as classified by the American College of Surgeons (ACS), typically involve surgeries where there is potential contamination from non-vital tissue or body fluids but no evidence of infection. Given that these types of wounds have some risk factors for surgical site infections due to their nature, yet not excessively high compared to other categories like heavily infected sites, it would be reasonable to expect an intermediate range rather than very low or very high percentages.

Answer Choice: B
The percentage of SSIs in patients with clean-contaminated wounds falls into this moderate-risk category, which aligns best with option B (<10%). This reflects a balanced likelihood of developing an infection without being overly concerning given standard care protocols. However, close monitoring post-surgery remains important to catch any early signs promptly.

Given that these types of wounds have some risk factors for surgical site infections due to their nature, yet not excessively high compared to other categories like heavily infected sites, it would be reasonable to expect an intermediate range rather than very low or very high percentages.

Answer Choice: B
The percentage of SSIs in patients with clean-contaminated wounds falls into this moderate-risk category, which aligns best with option B (<10%).
